sections.paragraph:
ἄνεσις, εως, ἡ (s. ἀνίημι; Hdt. 5, 28 et al.; SIG 880, 53; 884, 16; pap [e.g. PGiss 59, 7 of relief fr. liturgy]; LXX; TestSol 6:10 P; TestBenj 2:5c; ApcEsdr 5, 10 p. 30, 4 Tdf.; EpArist 284) ‘relaxing, loosening’.

① lit. relaxation of custodial control, some liberty, ἀ. ἔχειν have some freedom Ac 24:23 (cp. Jos., Ant. 18, 235 φυλακὴ μὲν γὰρ καὶ τήρησις ἦν, μετὰ μέντοι ἀνέσεως).

② relief from someth. onerous or troublesome, rest, relaxation, relief (Pla., Leg. 4, 724a; Strabo 10, 3, 9; M. Ant. 1, 16, 6; Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 156) ἵνα ἄλλοις ἄ. (sc. ᾖ ), ὑμῖν θλῖψις that others should have relief, and you be burdened 2 Cor 8:13. ἀ. ἔχειν (Jos., Bell. 3, 319) 2:13; 7:5. ε̣ἰ̣ς [ἄ]ν̣ε̣σ̣ι̣[ν]|τὸ λοιπὸν τὰ δάκρυα αὐτοῦ γενέ[ς]θαι so that his tears finally brought relief AcPl Ha 6, 5f. ἄνεσιν διδόναι τινί (Diod S 19, 26, 10 αὑτῷ δοὺς ἄνεσιν; 2 Ch 23:15; 1 Esdr 4:62; Jos., Ant. 3, 254; 281) B 4:2. ἀνταποδοῦναι τ. θλιβομένοις ἄνεσιν grant, in turn, rest to those who are oppressed 2 Th 1:7.—DELG s.v. ἵημι. M-M. TW.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

  1. fr. = from

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

someth. someth. = something

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 77
